codeReview.summary: Solid implementation covering all required states, API client error handling, chrome.storage caching, and mark-applied flow. The API client correctly distinguishes 401/403/network/timeout errors with typed error classes. The popup auto-reads the active tab URL, renders 10 states (loading, new, applied, duplicate, manual, unparseable, unsupported, auth-401, auth-403, network-error), handles mark-applied with optional expandable fields, caches results in chrome.storage.local, and refreshes in background. HTML has no inline scripts (MV3 CSP compliant). 16 well-structured vitest tests cover the API client module. Root vitest.config.ts properly excludes extension tests.

issues.description: AuthError.status is typed as '401 | 403' but is only ever constructed with 401 (403 uses ForbiddenError). The type union is misleading and creates a dead code path in popup.ts error handling where `err instanceof AuthError && err.status === 403` can never be true.

The condition `(err instanceof AuthError && err.status === 403)` is dead code since AuthError is never instantiated with status 403 — ForbiddenError handles that case. The `err instanceof ForbiddenError` check on the same line is sufficient.

When a cached result is shown immediately and the background re-check returns a different status, the UI flashes from one state to another without any transition or indication. For example, a stale 'already_applied' cache could flash to 'new'. This matches the spec ('show cached, then re-check') but may be jarring UX. Consider adding a subtle re-check indicator or diffing the result before re-rendering.

No test coverage for the .catch() fallback paths in handleResponse (lines 121, 128, 134 of api-client.ts) where response.json() throws on malformed JSON. These paths provide sensible defaults and are unlikely to regress, but adding a test for a non-JSON error response would improve coverage.

dangerouslyIgnoreUnhandledErrors: true is used to work around fake-timers + AbortController micro-task timing. The comment explains it well, but this flag suppresses ALL unhandled rejections in tests, which could mask real bugs in future tests added to this suite.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The extension-worker skill doesn't mention the need to create a root vitest.config.ts that excludes extension/ tests, even though the worker identified this as a suggested change. Without this, root `npm test` would pick up extension tests that depend on extension-specific mocks/config and fail.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The extension-worker skill could mention the need to create a root vitest.config.ts to exclude extension tests from the root test suite, since the root project picks up all *.test.ts files by default.' Root vitest.config.ts now excludes extension/**: vitest.config.ts line 4.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The extension-worker skill mentions dangerouslyIgnoreUnhandledErrors may be needed in extension vitest config for AbortController/fake-timer interaction in timeout tests. This is not documented anywhere but the worker discovered and applied it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: extension/vitest.config.ts uses dangerouslyIgnoreUnhandledErrors: true with an explanatory comment about fake timers + AbortController micro-task timing.

issues.description: AuthError.status typed as '401 | 403' but only instantiated with 401 — creates dead code path in popup.ts error handling
Dead code: `err instanceof AuthError && err.status === 403` can never be true since 403 uses ForbiddenError
UI can flash between cached and fresh state without transition when background re-check returns different status
No tests for malformed JSON response .catch() fallback paths in handleResponse
dangerouslyIgnoreUnhandledErrors: true suppresses all unhandled rejections, could mask future test bugs

summary: PASS. The extension popup UI and API client implementation is thorough and well-structured. All 10 popup states are correctly implemented with appropriate UI. The API client properly handles auth (401/403), network, timeout, and general errors with distinct typed error classes. Mark-applied flow works with optional expandable fields and cache invalidation. Chrome.storage caching with background re-check is implemented per spec. HTML is MV3 CSP compliant (no inline scripts). 16 vitest tests provide good API client coverage. Five non-blocking issues identified: misleading AuthError type union, dead code in error handling, cache-to-fresh flash UX, missing malformed JSON test coverage, and broad error suppression in vitest config. No blocking issues.
